title: Race still matters: the (post) racial politics of conspiracy theories
author: Ghumkhor, Gillespie, McMorrow

opening_move.description: The paper opens by diagnosing the proliferation of racist conspiracy theories (Great Replacement, White Genocide, QAnon, Eurabia) in liberal-democratic societies as a phenomenon that standard 'post-truth' explanations fail to account for.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: The dominant post-truth explanation reproduces a postracial obfuscation that conceals the structural-racial conditions enabling conspiracy theories in liberal democracies

theoretical_positioning.description: The paper argues against both post-truth and postracial frameworks, proposing a Lacanian psychoanalytic reading of conspiracy theories as symptoms of a repressed racial contract structuring liberal democracy.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: Lacanian psychoanalytic theory; critical race theory; postcolonial analysis; conceptual analysis of conspiracy theories
epistemic_mode.notes: Despite the subject matter (empirical phenomena: conspiracy theories, racial politics), the argument is entirely theoretical-interpretive. Examples (QAnon, Great Replacement) are used illustratively to instantiate the theoretical framework, not as systematic empirical evidence.

case_type.case_name: QAnon, Great Replacement Theory, White Genocide, Eurabia conspiracy theories

confidence_notes: The paper uses conspiracy theories as illustrative examples to make a theoretical claim about the racial structure of liberal democracy. The epistemic mode is normative-interpretive, not empirical, despite engaging empirical phenomena.
